messages.content:
What was the main emphasis of the Citizens United ruling in relation to free speech?

 

Group of answer choices

Free-speech rights were granted to corporations not because of their corporate status but because of their for-profit status.

Free-speech rights were granted to corporations because they can take the form of people.

Free-speech rights were granted to corporations because they make enormous contributions to society.

Free-speech rights were granted to corporations not because of their personhood but to support the rights of shareholders and other listeners.
Free-speech rights were granted to corporations because they can take the form of people.

Critics point out the Hobby Lobby ruling, though a narrow decision, could be used as precedent in future cases. How could this ruling be applied in a future case?

Group of answer choices

using corporate religious rights to justify a refusal to cover other controversial treatments

using corporate religious rights to promote social interest primacy

using corporate rights to promote other religious freedoms

using corporate rights to value religion over money
using corporate religious rights to justify a refusal to cover other controversial treatments.

What is a fiduciary?

Group of answer choices

a person who holds the job of a bank teller

a person accused of insider trading

a professional who breaches contracts

a person trusted by others because of his knowledge
a person trusted by others because of his knowledge.

What is both nonrivalrous and nonexclusive?

Group of answer choices

a public good

individual ownership

property management

a private good
a public good

When a business breaches a contract or fails to disclose material facts, this is called ______.

Group of answer choices

credit

tippee

fraud

twisting
fraud

What is the theme expressed by critics of the majority decision in the Citizens United case?

Group of answer choices

There are significant differences between corporations and individuals.

The contributions of corporations should be marginalized to protect listeners.

Shareholder primacy is at risk when corporations disseminate political information.

Social and political interests are best served by individuals, not corporations.
Social and political interests are best served by individuals, not corporations.

Which is an ethical issue faced by the pharmaceutical industry?

Group of answer choices

pharmaceutical companies not being focused enough on shareholder wealth

excluding people who need, but cannot afford, lifesaving medication

patients donating too much money for research and not enough researchers to use it

the regulatory infrastructure that governs the industry
excluding people who need, but cannot afford, lifesaving medication.

Because one cannot own an idea, legal protection is given only to the _________ of the idea.

Group of answer choices

artistic expression

original expression

creative expressive

copied expression
original expression

What was the biggest challenge for the prosecution in the Stewart Insider Trading case?

Group of answer choices

contradictions in testimony

protecting Stewart’s career

proving personal gain

proving insider information was shared
proving insider information was shared.

What does it mean to say that there is an asymmetry between the seller and the buyer?

Group of answer choices

The buyer knows more than the seller about a given product.

The market is tilted in favor of the seller instead of the buyer.

The government favors buyers over sellers in market outcomes.

The seller knows more than the buyer about a given product.
The seller knows more than the buyer about a given product.

Why did the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) launch an investigation that led to the Texas Gulf Sulphur case?

 

 

Group of answer choices

because there was speculation about the scale of operations

because more drilling was required for proper evaluation of the prospect

because the press prematurely shared nonpublic information with outsiders

because insiders bought shares of Texas Gulf while knowing about secret drilling activities
because insiders bought shares of Texas Gulf while knowing about secret drilling activities.

Companies have a(n) ______ to avoid or restrict products that are highly dangerous.

Group of answer choices

commitment

policy

obligation

purpose
obligation

How does the McCain-Feingold law impact a company, such as Citizens United, prepared to use its general funds to advertise and distribute a movie about a political candidate right before an election?

Group of answer choices

The law provides loopholes for the unconstitutional effects of the Bipartisan Campaign Reform Act of 2002.

The law allows a company to use its general funds to challenge the rules governing PACs.

The law prohibits a company from using general funds to release such a movie immediately before an election.

The law encourages a company to use its general funds to release such as movie immediately before an election
The law prohibits a company from using general funds to release such a movie immediately before an election.

Donald, a metallurgical engineer whose company provides tools for the pharmaceutical industry, created a new series of steps for melting certain types of metals. What kind of legal protection can he seek for his process?

Group of answer choices

process patent

utility patent

designed patent

product patent
process patent

What is the role of patents in Big Pharma?

 

Group of answer choices

Patents constrain competition and lead to higher prices.

Patents create competition and lower prices.

Patents limit access for patients.

Patents make drugs more affordable for patients.
Patents constrain competition and lead to higher prices.

The Supreme Court exempted Hobby Lobby from providing several contraceptives mandated by the Affordable Care Act. Based on this decision, one can conclude that ______.

Group of answer choices

the Supreme Court is biased against corporations

Hobby Lobby qualifies as a person who can exercise the freedom of religion

Hobby Lobby agrees with the mandates of the Affordable Care Act

the Supreme Court is biased against individual rights
Hobby Lobby qualifies as a person who can exercise the freedom of religion.

Companies use _____ to appeal to people’s need for social acceptability.

Group of answer choices

consumer autonomy

deception

advertising

due care
advertising

What is the role of an attorney who provides professional services to a corporation and is expected to keep insider nonpublic information confidential?

 

Group of answer choices

self-dealing

temporary insider

outsider

tippee
temporary insider
